meta.descriptor: DUBLIN, IRELAND → SYDNEY, NSW · AGENTIC AI RESTAURANT OS
meta.keyQuestion: Industry associations and investors relevant to my company

cover.kicker: MARKET ENTRY REPORT · IRISH SAAS ENTERING AUSTRALIA
cover.headline: Australia's restaurants have already adopted AI. The operating-system layer is still open.
cover.scope: Nory's agentic AI — autonomous demand prediction, staffing plans, inventory and payroll — maps directly onto what Sydney operations leaders are actively spending to solve.

narrative: Australia's hospitality market is valued at **USD 37.45B in 2025**, projected to USD 70.93B by 2034 {chip:sourced} — and AI adoption is mainstream: **65% of restaurants** deployed some form of AI, 42% of SMB venues specifically to manage staff costs {chip:sourced}. Clients such as **Dave's Hot Chicken, Oakberry and Black Sheep Coffee** prove Nory serves exactly the multi-location brands proliferating across Sydney.

The **EU–Australia FTA** (concluded March 2026) eliminates 99%+ of tariffs and opens digital trade to Irish companies {chip:sourced}; Enterprise Ireland and IDA Ireland actively support the corridor. Sydney/NSW addressable opportunity: **low hundreds of millions AUD** {chip:est}. Near-term levers: Pty Ltd incorporation unlocking the **43.5% R&DTI**, SOC 2 / ISO 27001, and Xero + MYOB connectors.
exec.keyQuestionAnswer: The two most actionable starting points: **Enterprise Ireland's Sydney office** and the **Restaurant & Catering Industry Association (R&CA)** — the peak foodservice body and primary gateway to operator networks. Key investor matches: [SproutX](/innovation-ecosystem/sproutx) (leading foodtech hub) and [Aura Ventures](/investors/aura-ventures) (Sydney VC targeting AI-enabled B2B software).

highlights.name: SproutX
highlights.why: Direct access to hospitality networks and specialised sector investors

highlights.name: Aura Ventures
highlights.why: AI-enabled B2B software thesis — aligned with Nory's agentic-AI profile

heroStat.label: MOST MATERIAL FINANCIAL INCENTIVE
heroStat.caption: Refundable R&D Tax Incentive on eligible AI/software development conducted in Australia — unlocked by incorporating a Pty Ltd.

sequence.label: THE REPORT'S ENTRY SEQUENCE

rows.text: Incorporate Pty Ltd, ABN, resident director; engage a local Head of Sales
Two to three anchor wins among Sydney multi-location restaurant groups
Reference clients accelerate POS/PMS channel partnerships; national expansion

sequence.caveat: A suggested shape, not a prescription — sequencing is the core agenda for your strategy session.

tiles.caption: Australia hospitality LMS software market, 2024 — adjacent training/compliance layer
Global AI-in-restaurants software revenue, 2025 (48.3% of total)
Australian restaurants already using some form of AI

metrics.footnote: Also sized: AU tour-operator software USD 30.60M @ 9.74% CAGR — a secondary adjacency.

strengths.text: Autonomous forecasting/staffing/payroll align with the AI-native trend of the 2026 AU landscape
Restaurant managers at AUD $75–95k make automation a hedge
EU–Australia FTA framework — e-signature recognition, no duties on digital products
P&L focus addresses the 42% of SMB venues seeking AI for staff costs

weaknesses.text: At least one director must ordinarily reside in Australia

opportunities.text: AUD $1.2B hospitality LMS adjacency for AI-augmented training
AI waste tracking cuts food waste up to 25% {chip:sourced}
MVP Ventures / ETCF-class programs (round status varies)

threats.text: Rents up 9.8% YoY, premium to AUD $1,492/sqm
Privacy Act 1988 + NDB scheme over AUD $3M turnover
AI/ML engineers AUD $130–180k+ vs incumbents and hyperscalers

competitors.intro: The AU hospitality stack is transitioning from fragmented point solutions to integrated AI ecosystems. The gap: a platform connecting predicted inventory needs directly to labour requirements — the 'Autonomous Restaurant Brain' sitting above existing tools.

you.name: Nory
you.position: Agentic AI operating system — demand, staffing, inventory, payroll in one
you.strengths: Autonomous actions, not just analytics; ingests POS data to drive decisions

rows.name: Ordermentum
rows.position: Dominant AU hospitality procurement; 'Australia's #1 platform for venues & suppliers'
rows.differs: Transactions, not P&L or payroll — also a channel to register on

rows.name: OrderMate
rows.position: Australian-born POS — the operational heartbeat of many NSW venues
rows.differs: No predictive agentic layer — Nory sits on top, not against

rows.name: SevenRooms
rows.differs: Front-of-house only — inventory and payroll left to others

rows.name: Prediko
rows.position: AI-driven inventory management and demand planning
rows.strengths: Raw materials + automated restocking; NSW 3PL integrations
rows.differs: Inventory only — no staffing or payroll

competitors.gaps: **1 · Award-wage complexity** — NSW groups struggle with award wages and payroll compliance. **2 · Inventory→labour in one workflow** — nobody connects them. **3 · The integrator seat** — value sits above the POS layer.
competitors.positioningRead: Market as **P&L Autopilot** — managing Australian labour costs and demand volatility — rather than competing as a POS. POS-partnership depth vs direct enterprise sales is a strategy-session discussion.
competitors.scanHookCopy: This table covers the five primary alternatives. We can profile adjacent players — Lightspeed, Deputy, Tanda, workforce-management point tools — on request.

accounts.intro: High-priority targets where Nory's operating system solves immediate operational complexity.

briefed.name: Merivale
briefed.signals: Expansion — new CBD freeholds, major mixed-use precinct; focus on resolving historical labour-compliance challenges
briefed.fit: Diverse concepts align with managing labour, inventory and performance in one platform
briefed.angle: Radical transparency in labour cost + automated compliance

briefed.name: Australian Venue Co
briefed.signals: Actively hiring; rapid acquisition and integration of new venues demands standardised ops tech
briefed.fit: Deployable quickly into acquired sites; consolidates scheduling and analytics estate-wide
briefed.angle: The integration layer for acquisitions — instant standardisation

unbriefed.name: Guzman y Gomez
unbriefed.reason: No verified website domain on intake — send the domain to unlock a full brief

accounts.worthKnowing: Registered clubs (RSLs, sporting clubs) operate massive high-margin hospitality footprints — reachable via the Australasian Gaming Expo. Enterprise procurement treats Xero/MYOB/Lightspeed integration and SOC 2 / ISO 27001 as baseline.

providers.intro: Given the entry sequence — entity setup, then R&DTI, then enterprise sales — the three below map to the first 90 days. All nine are credible matches.

ourRead.name: MinterEllison
ourRead.why: Foreign investment and employment law for the incorporation: Pty Ltd via ASIC Form 201 (AUD $3–8k + ~$660), resident-director structuring, Privacy Act compliance.

ourRead.name: Fullstack Advisory
ourRead.why: R&DTI consulting and virtual CFO — the named route to the 43.5% offset, plus double-tax-treaty structuring.

ourRead.name: ADAPT
ourRead.why: A/NZ customer intelligence; qualified lead lists and invitation-only executive roundtables — direct access to enterprise operations leaders.

all.name: NEXTGEN Group
all.description: North Sydney growth engine — Sales as a Service and reseller networks; a named answer to the distribution challenge.

all.name: Stone & Chalk
all.description: Australia's largest innovation community — mentorship, investor access, suggested first base over CBD leases.

all.name: TechVisa
all.description: Tech-sector visa specialists — 482/408 assessment for relocating Irish staff.

all.name: Accenture Australia
all.description: Systems-integration muscle for multi-site deals needing PMS/ERP connectivity.

all.name: EverestEngineering
all.description: Product localisation and AI data enablement in the AU technical landscape.

all.name: Granton
all.description: Grant matching and writing — EMDG and open NSW innovation rounds.

gov.name: Investment NSW
gov.why: State agency for international landings — matchmaking, site selection, government liaison; engage early

gov.name: Irish Australian Chamber of Commerce
gov.why: Bilateral networking — the direct channel into the Irish-Australian business community

gov.name: Export Connect
gov.why: Sydney F&B consultancy — pre-qualified buyers and AU SaaS pricing benchmarking

hubs.name: SproutX
hubs.description: National foodtech accelerator; mentors and investors across the hospitality supply chain. Flagged in your key-question answer.

hubs.name: UNSW Founders 10x
hubs.description: Sydney flagship accelerator; seed investment and capital-raise support.

hubs.name: Haymarket HQ
hubs.description: Central-Sydney hub; scale-up/corporate/investor networking; co-working alternative.

hubs.name: H2 Ventures
hubs.description: AI/data specialist; regulatory guidance and a dedicated VC network.

govAndHubs.alsoNamed: Also named: **Enterprise Ireland (Sydney office)**, **Austrade**, **R&CA** (57,000+ venues), **Australian Hotels Association**, and the **Lansdowne Club** — Sydney's premier Irish-Australian business network.

mentors.intro: All five have Irish-Australian corridor experience — mitigating exactly the risks the SWOT flags: local hiring, pricing localisation, payments regulation.

primary.name: Ben Morrisroe
primary.why: The most direct match: advises on localising payroll and inventory modules — Nory's two core surfaces.

primary.name: Ronan Lehane
primary.why: Fundraising within the Irish-Australian corridor — sounding board before the investor list.

primary.name: Navaid Khatib
primary.why: Australian payments regulation — payroll automation crosses into regulated territory.

extra.name: Dónal Brady
extra.why: Remote-work compliance and international team scaling for Dublin↔Sydney.

extra.name: Ian Corbally
extra.why: Irish founder; refining technical infrastructure costs in AU.

investors.intro: The research suggests one possible order — sector specialists, growth partners, broad tech VCs in parallel — but which doors first is the strategy-session conversation.

approachOrder.label: Sector specialists first
approachOrder.text: [EVP](/investors/evp) (B2B SaaS + AI-first; AU$500k–5M) and angel [Sam Cust](/investors/sam-cust) (hospitality/food-services) — domain expertise to validate local P&L and pricing.

approachOrder.label: Growth partners
approachOrder.text: [Potentia Capital](/investors/potentia-capital) and [Grok Ventures](/investors/grok-ventures) for larger tranches behind hiring and national distribution.

approachOrder.label: Broad tech VCs in parallel
approachOrder.text: [Aura Ventures](/investors/aura-ventures) (AU$500k–2M — key-question highlight), [Shearwater Capital](/investors/shearwater-capital), [Investible](/investors/investible) for competitive tension and ANZ backing.

all.name: EVP
all.description: Sydney; B2B SaaS and AI-first software — highest-conviction thesis match.

all.name: Potentia Capital
all.description: Growth equity and buyouts for tech-enabled services.

all.name: Sam Cust
all.description: Hospitality & food-services specialist; local industry connections.

all.name: Albert Patajo
all.description: Sydney operator-investor; B2B SaaS and marketplaces.

cards.name: Australasian Gaming Expo
cards.why: The gateway to the clubs-and-pubs sector — high-volume multi-location operations, prime candidates for automated staffing and inventory.

cards.name: Big Data & AI Summit Australia
cards.why: Tech investors and enterprise partners — position agentic AI against legacy POS with Fair Work compliance as the lead message.

cards.name: Australian Tourism Exchange
cards.why: Australia's largest B2B tourism event — plus the Irish-Australian expat network for warm distribution leads.

events.alsoFlagged: Also flagged: Fine Food Australia (Sydney, September) and ADAPT CIO Edge events.

actionPlan.intro: Three phases over six months. The default map — pace and emphasis set together in the strategy session.

phases.title: Legal, compliance & entity setup
phases.body: Incorporate Pty Ltd via [MinterEllison](/service-providers/minterellison) · resident director; 482/408 via [TechVisa](/service-providers/techvisa) · ABN, GST, TFN · Privacy Act: data flows, DPA, NDB · IP Australia trademarks · register R&DTI immediately · contact [Investment NSW](/government-support/investment-nsw) · setup outlay AUD $10–20k ex-rent.

phases.title: Team, space & network
phases.body: Base at [Stone & Chalk](/service-providers/stone-and-chalk) or [Haymarket HQ](/innovation-ecosystem/haymarket-hq) · hire Sydney Head of Sales (AUD $120–160k + super) · [Fullstack Advisory](/service-providers/fullstack-advisory) for CFO/R&DTI · [Irish Australian Chamber](/government-support/irish-australian-chamber-of-commerce) + Enterprise Ireland · [Export Connect](/government-support/export-connect) pricing benchmark · join [ADAPT](/service-providers/adapt); explore [UNSW Founders 10x](/innovation-ecosystem/unsw-founders-10x), [H2 Ventures](/innovation-ecosystem/h2-ventures).

phases.title: Go-to-market & sales
phases.body: Priority: [Merivale](/accounts/merivale) and [AVC](/accounts/australian-venue-co) · integrate Xero, MYOB, Lightspeed before outbound · SOC 2 / ISO 27001 · [NEXTGEN Group](/service-providers/nextgen-group) distribution · [Accenture](/service-providers/accenture-australia) / [EverestEngineering](/service-providers/everestengineering) SI support · [SproutX](/innovation-ecosystem/sproutx) + [Granton](/service-providers/granton) grants · pipeline at Fine Food Australia · lead with labour-savings and waste ROI.

compliance.intro: The Australia–Ireland double tax treaty streamlines the path, but early planning avoids the slow steps.

table.finding: At least one director ordinarily resident in Australia; short-term visa holders don't qualify
table.note: Local hire or nominee director; Director ID from Ireland takes 4–6 weeks — start first

table.finding: GST over A$75k turnover; corporate 30%/25% vs Ireland 15%
table.note: Treaty prevents double taxation; dividends capped ~15%; R&DTI 43.5% on AU R&D

table.finding: NDB scheme over A$3M turnover; payroll data high-sensitivity
table.note: Data flows, DPA, OAIC breach procedures before the first enterprise deal

table.finding: NES + awards set minimum pay; 482 TSS primary relocation visa
table.note: EOR (e.g. Deel) validates market pre-incorporation; digital-first banking opens faster

stats.caption: Total setup outlay — legal, registration, compliance (ex-rent)

checklist.text: Baseline for enterprise RFP security review
The accounting-stack filter; Lightspeed on POS
Payroll must handle Fair Work awards natively
IP Australia early, before brand spend

guides.intro: Each answers a question this report raises — cultural fit, partner-led distribution, enterprise AI positioning.

cards.title: How Starbucks struggled in the Australian market
cards.summary: Misjudging the independent café culture — the cost of imported operating assumptions.
cards.relevantBecause: Your buyers ARE that culture — localisation isn't optional.

cards.title: How Shopify won Australian merchants with partner-led growth
cards.summary: Penetration through a local partner ecosystem.
cards.relevantBecause: The report's distribution read follows this model.

cards.title: How Anthropic entered the Australian market
cards.relevantBecause: Maps to the Merivale / AVC tier of your account list.

cards.title: How UiPath entered the Australian market
cards.summary: Deep technical partnerships; agentic AI in the AU landscape.
cards.relevantBecause: The closest precedent — agentic automation sold into AU enterprise.

dataset.name: Hospitality & Tourism Operators — Australia
dataset.description: GMs and technology decision-makers across Australian hotel groups, with technology-readiness scores to identify Sydney early adopters.

leads.customBuildCopy: Need more granular focus — high-volume QSR groups, Irish-owned businesses in Australia, or NSW multi-venue operators by tech stack, turnover or headcount? Our research team curates bespoke lists.

close.headline: This report is the map. The route gets chosen together.
close.body: What it deliberately doesn't do is lock in strategy: sequencing incorporation against first sales, choosing the anchor account, and which investors and mentors to engage first are worked through in your included 60-minute strategy session.
arriveWith: EOR-first market validation vs immediate Pty Ltd incorporation
Merivale or AVC as the anchor pursuit — depth vs scale
POS partnership (OrderMate-class) vs pure direct enterprise sales
Which two investors and which two mentors to approach first

analyst: AU hospitality LMS + tour-operator software sizing
Sydney CBD rent and salary benchmarks
vendor: Ordermentum, OrderMate, SevenRooms, Netstock, Prediko materials
